patches.reason: The published global /v2/hacktivity endpoint returns the global feed even when a single program slug is passed, while the YesWeHack web app uses /programs/{slug}/hacktivity for project activity.
patches.validated_outcome: Digital Flanders project hacktivity returned 706 rows via the project-specific endpoint.

patches.summary: Generate deterministic row-hash local IDs for hacktivity rows that do not include an upstream primary key.
patches.reason: Hacktivity sync fetched rows but stored zero because each row lacked id, slug, name, uuid, or another generic fallback key.
patches.validated_outcome: sync --resources hacktivity --latest-only stored 50 rows without no-extractable-ID warnings; hacktivity trends and learn returned filtered project rows.

patches.summary: Normalize program hacktivity item envelopes to arrays before output and caching.
patches.reason: The project-specific endpoint can return an items/pagination envelope for single-page reads, while the table renderer and --all path expect a top-level array.
patches.validated_outcome: programs hacktivity <slug> returns a consistent array shape for single-page and --all reads.
